incidents.vector: Exploited a vulnerability in Kelp DAO's rsETH bridge and LayerZero cross-chain integration to withdraw restaked Ether tokens.
incidents.impact: Approximately $292 million in crypto assets drained, making it the largest crypto heist of 2026 to date.

incidents.vector: Purpose-built OT malware (ZionSiphon) designed to detect Israeli water/desalination systems and push unsafe pump-pressure and chlorine settings.
incidents.impact: Darktrace found the sabotage malware failed to trigger due to a coding bug, so no disruption occurred; it demonstrates continuing Iranian intent against Israeli water infrastructure.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to Vercel customer data, later claimed for sale on a criminal forum by the ShinyHunters group.
incidents.impact: A limited set of customer data was exposed; Vercel notified affected customers and urged rotation of keys and secrets.

incidents.vector: Unspecified intrusion that prompted a precautionary shutdown of city IT networks; neighboring Leon County disconnected its network, consistent with ransomware containment.
incidents.impact: Multiple city websites and public services went offline over the weekend and remained degraded.

incidents.victim: Global Dragon Boss adware victims (24K+ systems)
incidents.industry: Cross-sector (incl. government, OT, higher ed, Fortune 500)
incidents.vector: A March 2025 update to Dragon Boss adware used the Advanced Installer update channel to disable ESET, McAfee, Kaspersky, and Malwarebytes and exclude future payloads from Windows Defender.
incidents.impact: Roughly 23,500 machines across 124 countries were primed for follow-on attacks, including 35 government entities, 41 OT networks, 221 universities, and several Fortune 500 firms; Huntress sinkholed the backup domain before attackers could deploy follow-on malware.

incidents.vector: Exploited a Salesforce platform misconfiguration to scrape non-sensitive customer data from a hosted McGraw Hill webpage, part of a broader ShinyHunters campaign against Salesforce tenants.
incidents.impact: Records for up to 13.5 million users (names, addresses, emails, phone numbers) were leaked after extortion was refused; no SSNs, financial data, or student records were affected.

incidents.victim: Ukrainian prosecutors, plus NATO-allied militaries (Romania, Greece, Bulgaria, Serbia)
incidents.vector: Long-running credential phishing and mailbox compromise operation targeting prosecutors and defense agencies; exposed when the operators inadvertently left logs accessible on the internet.
incidents.impact: At least 284 inboxes compromised between September 2024 and March 2026, including 170+ Ukrainian investigator accounts, 67 Romanian Air Force mailboxes, 27 Hellenic Defense General Staff mailboxes, and senior anti-corruption officials.

incidents.vector: Attempted intrusion into the plant's operational technology (OT) environment, which controls physical infrastructure at the facility.
incidents.impact: The attack was stopped by built-in safeguards and caused no operational impact; Swedish officials said similar attempts have been observed in Norway, Denmark, and on a larger scale in Poland.

incidents.vector: Booby-trapped PDFs exploited a zero-day (CVE-2026-34621) that chains improper input validation with unsafe object handling to execute privileged Acrobat APIs on opening.
incidents.impact: Attackers have been exploiting the flaw since at least November 2025 to fingerprint systems, exfiltrate files, and stage follow-on RCE/sandbox-escape payloads; Adobe patched the flaw on April 11.

incidents.vector: A GitHub Actions workflow misconfiguration caused OpenAI to pull a malicious version of the Axios JavaScript library compromised on March 31 in a North Korea-linked supply chain attack.
incidents.impact: The workflow had access to the certificate and notarization material used to sign OpenAI macOS apps (ChatGPT Desktop, Codex, Atlas); OpenAI assesses the signing certificate was not successfully exfiltrated and no user data was accessed, but is forcing all macOS users to update.

incidents.vector: Intrusion into the city attorney's systems; method not disclosed.
incidents.impact: 7.7 TB of data was published online, including sensitive case details, witness names, medical records, internal affairs investigations, and personal information of LAPD officers.

incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — financial fraud (tracked as Storm-2755)
incidents.vector: Malvertising and SEO-poisoned Microsoft 365 sign-in pages harvested credentials and session tokens via adversary-in-the-middle, then Storm-2755 impersonated victims to request direct-deposit changes in Workday.
incidents.impact: Token replay via the Axios client let the actor bypass legacy MFA and persist undetected; at least one Canadian worker's paycheck was successfully redirected to an attacker-controlled account.

incidents.vector: Intrusion by a group calling itself FlamingChina that surfaced in February 2026; specific access method not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Petabytes of sensitive data were leaked, including files marked 'secret' that appear to contain Chinese military and government material; investigators took weeks to confirm the dump's authenticity due to its size.

incidents.victim: Global SOHO router owners — 200+ organizations, 5,000+ consumer devices, 18,000+ routers across 120+ countries
incidents.industry: Government, Military, Critical Infrastructure, IT, Telecom, Energy
incidents.vector: GRU actors exploited known vulnerabilities in TP-Link and Mikrotik SOHO routers, pointed DNS to actor-controlled resolvers, and ran adversary-in-the-middle attacks against Microsoft Outlook Web Access to harvest passwords, OAuth tokens, and emails.
incidents.impact: Russia harvested credentials and tokens from targets in 23+ US states, Europe, Afghanistan, North Africa, Central America, and Southeast Asia; DOJ Operation Masquerade court-authorized takedown on April 7 reset DNS on US routers and disrupted the campaign.

incidents.victim: US water, wastewater, energy, and local-government operators
incidents.vector: Iranian actors targeted internet-exposed Rockwell/Allen-Bradley PLCs (notably CVE-2021-22681) and Siemens devices, manipulating HMI/SCADA displays and project files.
incidents.impact: FBI, NSA, CISA, EPA, DOE, and Cyber Command confirmed 'operational disruption and financial loss' at unnamed US critical infrastructure since March 2026; a Minot, ND water-treatment plant was hit with a ransomware-style incident, and Iranian activity is escalating alongside the US-Iran conflict.

incidents.vector: Ransomware intrusion at ChipSoft, which forced the vendor to disable Zorgportaal, HiX Mobile, and the Zorgplatform while it rebuilds and reissues credentials.
incidents.impact: Systems were disconnected at 11+ Dutch hospitals, logistics and patient-communication workflows were degraded, and LUMC delayed rollout of a new ChipSoft EHR; ChipSoft cannot rule out patient-data access. HiX is used by roughly 70% of Dutch hospitals.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack on the centralized C2K school IT network operated by the Education Authority with service provider Capita; specific intrusion method not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Access disrupted for up to 300,000 pupils and 20,000 teachers across the vast majority of schools in Northern Ireland; EA is staging password resets and prioritizing students facing exams, but data-leakage review is ongoing.

incidents.vector: Successful phishing attack against the firm, later confirmed by Jones Day after attackers posted client materials online.
incidents.impact: Attackers accessed client files at one of the largest US corporate law firms (2,400+ lawyers), exposing privileged communications for a client base that includes major corporations and political campaigns.
